MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues By Defendant Jimmy Lui
Off Calendar Oct-09-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. J. KEVIN MORRISON, ESQ. of Jones Clifford, Tel No. 415-431-5310 appearing for Plaintiff Ava Knox;
Atty. RICHARD B. WILBUR, ESQ. of Williams Pinelli & Cullen, LLP, Tel No. 408-288-3868 appearing for Defendant Jimmy Lui.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JIMMY LUI'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or Alternatively, Summary Adjudication Of Issues By Defendant Jimmy Lui is denied as a matter of law. Because the eviction was not complete and the unlawful detainer judgment was not satisfied at the time of the accident, defendant continued to legally possess and control the premises. Accordingly, defendant owed a duty to plaintiff when the injury occurred. (Martinez v. Bank of America Nat. Trust & Sav. Ass'n (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 883, 892-893). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Sep-11-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Atty. JOSHUA D. WHITE, ESQ. of Jones, Clifford, Johnson, Dehner, Wong, Morrison, Sheppard & Bell, LLP, Tel No. 415-431-5310 appearing for the Plaintiff;
Atty. KEVIN T. DUNBAR, ESQ. of Dunbar & Associates APLC, Tel No. 562-491-7600 appearing for the Defendant JPMorgan Chase Bank, N.A.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT JPMORGAN CHASE BANK, N.A.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is granted. Defendant did not owe a duty of care to plaintiff as a matter of law. See Martinez v. Bank of America Nat. Trust & Sav. Ass'n (2000) 82 Cal.App.4th 883, 892-893. Until the unlawful detainer judgment was obtained and satisfied, defendant lacked the ability to control the conditions on the premises. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
